session_1.scenario: A recent situation i wish had unfolded differently happened during a team meeting at work we were discussing the progress of a project that had been falling behind schedule and tensions were already a bit high because leadership had started asking for updates more frequently i had been quietly frustrated for a few weeks because i felt like one of my teammates was not pulling their weight and i had taken on extra tasks to keep things moving without directly addressing it with them during the meeting our manager asked why certain milestones had not been completed

LLM-CT.item: I could have addressed my concerns with my teammate privately as soon as I noticed I was taking on extra tasks, clarified responsibilities, and agreed on a plan before the team meeting.

LLM-CF.item: I could have spoken with my teammate privately as soon as I noticed I was taking on extra work, clarified responsibilities, and raised any ongoing gap to our manager before the meeting.

LLM-B.item: I could have addressed my concerns with my teammate privately and early on, clarifying responsibilities and asking for a plan to rebalance the workload before the meeting.

session_2.scenario: a situation i wish had unfolded differently happened a few months ago during a conversation with  a close family member about planning a holiday gathering what started as a simple discussion about dates and responsibilities gradually turned into a tense exchange that left both of us feeling frustrated and misunderstood and we were trying to coordinate who would host and what each person would contribute and i had already been bit irritable when my family member suggested that i host  the event because of my home had more space i immediately felt stressed instead of calmly explaining that i did not feel able to take on that responsibility this year i responded defensively

LLM-CT.item: I could have paused before responding and calmly said that I was feeling too stretched to host this year, then suggested we look together at another host or split the responsibilities differently.

LLM-CF.item: I could have paused before responding and calmly said that I was feeling too stretched to host this year, then suggested we look at another host or split responsibilities differently.

LLM-B.item: I could have taken a breath and calmly said that I wasn’t able to host this year, while offering to help in another way.

session_3.scenario: As the week went on the extra assignment turned out to be more time consuming than i had anticipated i started staying late skipping breaks and working through lunch to try to keep my stress level increased and i began to feel overwhelmed and frustrated instead of communicating  this to my manager i kept pushing myself to handle everything on my own at the time i did not want to appear unhelpful or incapable i also assumed i could figure it out and make it work by outting in extra time what i did not to do was pause to assess my current workload or ask for clarification about the timeline and expectations for this new task
User_Plans_Final: suggest to another team mates who might have availability to assist

LLM-CT.item: I could have told my manager as soon as the extra assignment started taking more time than expected that my workload was becoming unmanageable, and asked to clarify priorities, deadlines, and whether anything could be reassigned so I would not keep overextending myself.

LLM-CF.item: I could have told my manager as soon as the extra assignment started taking more time than expected that my workload was becoming unmanageable and asked to clarify priorities and deadlines instead of silently working longer hours.

LLM-B.item: I could have told my manager early that the extra assignment was more than I could realistically handle with my current workload and asked to clarify priorities, timeline, and support.

feedback.1: no not anything to find a like or dislike because all are useful information to get at right time

feedback.3: actions and based on a responsibilities to find everything
